232:### US-GTD-4: Manage Projects
233-
234-**As a** GTD practitioner,
235-**I want to** see all my active projects and their next actions,
236-**So that** I can ensure every project is moving forward.
237-
238-**Acceptance Criteria:**
239:- Projects list shows all active projects
240-- Each project shows its title and the count of next actions
241-- User can view a project to see all its linked next actions and completed actions
242:- Projects without a next action are flagged (stalled projects)
243-- User can add a project directly (without going through inbox)
244-- User can add a next action directly to a project
245-- User can complete a project (marks it as finished)
--
304-### US-GTD-8: Navigation and GTD Dashboard
305-
306-**As a** GTD practitioner,
307:**I want to** navigate between Inbox, Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For, and
308-Someday/Maybe,
309-**So that** I can access any part of my GTD system quickly.
310-

313-  Someday/Maybe, Weekly Review
314-- Dashboard/home page shows a summary: inbox count, stalled projects count, next actions count
315-- Navigation is accessible (keyboard, screen reader) with current page indicated
--

326-1. A user can capture items to their inbox and process them to zero
327:2. Clarified items land in the correct list (Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For,

329-3. Next Actions are filterable by context so users can work from context-appropriate lists

file.content:
### US-GTD-4: Manage Projects

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** see all my active projects and their next actions,
**So that** I can ensure every project is moving forward.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Projects list shows all active projects
- Each project shows its title and the count of next actions
- User can view a project to see all its linked next actions and completed actions
- Projects without a next action are flagged (stalled projects)
- User can add a project directly (without going through inbox)
- User can add a next action directly to a project
- User can complete a project (marks it as finished)
- User can delete a project
- Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances

**Success Metric:** During weekly review, user can verify all projects have next actions
in under 2 minutes.

### US-GTD-5: Waiting For List

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** track items I'm waiting on from others,
**So that** I can follow up at the right time and nothing falls through the cracks.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Waiting For list shows all items the user is waiting on
- Each item shows: what is being waited on, who/what it's waiting on, date added
- User can add items directly to Waiting For

144→---
   145→
   146→## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)
   147→
   148→*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
   149→team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
   150→implementation.*
   151→
   152→### Core GTD Concepts
   153→
   154→| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
   155→|-------------|-------------------|-------|
   156→| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
   157→| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
   158→| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
   159→| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
   160→| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
   161→| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
   162→| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (text), and date added. |
   163→| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
   164→| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
   165→| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
   166→
   167→### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
   168→
   169→| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
   170→|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
   171→| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
   172→| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
   173→| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
   174→| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
   175→| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
   176→| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
   177→| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
   178→| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   179→| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   180→| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
   181→| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
   182→| Drop project | `Project::drop()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned |
   183→| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
   184→| Resolve waiting for | `resolve()` | WaitingForItem -> completed or moved to inbox |
   185→| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
   186→| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |
   187→
   188→### GTD State Machines
   189→
   190→**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
   191→```
   192→                capture()
   193→  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
   194→                                │
   195→                      clarify() │
   196→              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
   197→              v        v        v        v        v
   198→         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
   199→```
   200→
   201→**NextAction Lifecycle:**
   202→```
   203→                  clarify_as_next_action()
   204→  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
   205→                                               │
   206→                                   complete()  │
   207→                                               v
   208→                                         NextAction (Completed)
   209→```
   210→
   211→**Project Lifecycle:**
   212→```
   213→                    clarify_as_project()
   214→  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
   215→                                           │  │
   216→                              complete()   │  │  drop()
   217→                                           v  v
   218→                                    Completed / Dropped

file.content:
---

## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)

*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
implementation.*

### Core GTD Concepts

| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (text), and date added. |
| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |

### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
| Drop project | `Project::drop()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned |
| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
| Resolve waiting for | `resolve()` | WaitingForItem -> completed or moved to inbox |
| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |

### GTD State Machines

**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
```
                capture()
  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
                                │
                      clarify() │
              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
              v        v        v        v        v
         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
```

**NextAction Lifecycle:**
```
                  clarify_as_next_action()
  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
                                               │
                                   complete()  │
                                               v
                                         NextAction (Completed)
```

**Project Lifecycle:**
```
                    clarify_as_project()
  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
                                           │  │
                              complete()   │  │  drop()
                                           v  v
                                    Completed / Dropped

1→use axum::Router;
     2→use sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions;
     3→use sqlx::PgPool;
     4→use tokio::net::TcpListener;
     5→use tower_http::request_id::{MakeRequestUuid, PropagateRequestIdLayer, SetRequestIdLayer};
     6→use tower_http::services::ServeDir;
     7→use tower_http::trace::TraceLayer;
     8→use tower_sessions::SessionManagerLayer;
     9→use tower_sessions_sqlx_store::PostgresStore;
    10→
    11→use crate::configuration::Settings;
    12→use crate::routes::{
    13→    get_contexts, get_dashboard, get_edit_context, get_edit_next_action, get_edit_todo,
    14→    get_forgot_password, get_inbox, get_login, get_next_action_item, get_next_actions,
    15→    get_projects, get_register, get_review, get_someday_maybe, get_todo_item, get_todos_page,
    16→    get_waiting_for, health_check, index, post_clarify_inbox_item, post_complete_next_action,
    17→    post_context, post_delete_context, post_delete_inbox_item, post_delete_next_action,
    18→    post_delete_todo, post_edit_context, post_edit_next_action, post_edit_todo, post_inbox,
    19→    post_login, post_logout, post_next_action, post_register, post_todo, post_toggle_todo,
    20→};
    21→
    22→pub struct Application {
    23→    listener: TcpListener,
    24→    pool: PgPool,
    25→}
    26→
    27→impl Application {
    28→    pub async fn build(settings: &Settings) -> Result<Self, anyhow::Error> {
    29→        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
    30→            .max_connections(5)
    31→            .connect_with(settings.database.connect_options())
    32→            .await?;
    33→
    34→        sqlx::migrate!("./migrations").run(&pool).await?;
    35→
    36→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    37→        session_store.migrate().await?;
    38→
    39→        let address = format!(
    40→            "{}:{}",
    41→            settings.application.host, settings.application.port
    42→        );
    43→        let listener = TcpListener::bind(&address).await?;
    44→
    45→        tracing::info!("Listening on {}", listener.local_addr()?);
    46→
    47→        Ok(Self { listener, pool })
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    pub fn port(&self) -> u16 {
    51→        self.listener.local_addr().unwrap().port()
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    pub fn router(pool: PgPool) -> Router {
    55→        let x_request_id = axum::http::HeaderName::from_static("x-request-id");
    56→
    57→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    58→        let session_layer = SessionManagerLayer::new(session_store);
    59→
    60→        Router::new()
    61→            .route("/", axum::routing::get(index))
    62→            .route("/dashboard", axum::routing::get(get_dashboard))
    63→            .route("/inbox", axum::routing::get(get_inbox).post(post_inbox))
    64→            .route(
    65→                "/inbox/{id}/delete",
    66→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_inbox_item),
    67→            )
    68→            .route(
    69→                "/inbox/{id}/clarify",
    70→                axum::routing::post(post_clarify_inbox_item),
    71→            )
    72→            .route(
    73→                "/contexts",
    74→                axum::routing::get(get_contexts).post(post_context),
    75→            )
    76→            .route(
    77→                "/contexts/{id}/edit",
    78→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_context).post(post_edit_context),
    79→            )
    80→            .route(
    81→                "/contexts/{id}/delete",
    82→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_context),
    83→            )
    84→            .route(
    85→                "/next-actions",
    86→                axum::routing::get(get_next_actions).post(post_next_action),
    87→            )
    88→            .route(
    89→                "/next-actions/{id}",
    90→                axum::routing::get(get_next_action_item),
    91→            )
    92→            .route(
    93→                "/next-actions/{id}/complete",
    94→                axum::routing::post(post_complete_next_action),
    95→            )
    96→            .route(
    97→                "/next-actions/{id}/delete",
    98→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_next_action),
    99→            )
   100→            .route(
   101→                "/next-actions/{id}/edit",
   102→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_next_action).post(post_edit_next_action),
   103→            )
   104→            .route("/projects", axum::routing::get(get_projects))
   105→            .route("/waiting-for", axum::routing::get(get_waiting_for))
   106→            .route("/someday-maybe", axum::routing::get(get_someday_maybe))
   107→            .route("/review", axum::routing::get(get_review))
   108→            .route(
   109→                "/register",
   110→                axum::routing::get(get_register).post(post_register),
   111→            )
   112→            .route("/login", axum::routing::get(get_login).post(post_login))
   113→            .route("/forgot-password", axum::routing::get(get_forgot_password))
   114→            .route("/logout", axum::routing::post(post_logout))
   115→            .route("/todos", axum::routing::get(get_todos_page).post(post_todo))
   116→            .route("/todos/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_todo_item))
   117→            .route("/todos/{id}/toggle", axum::routing::post(post_toggle_todo))
   118→            .route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo))
   119→            .route(
   120→                "/todos/{id}/edit",
   121→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_todo).post(post_edit_todo),
   122→            )
   123→            .route("/health_check", axum::routing::get(health_check))
   124→            .nest_service("/static", ServeDir::new("static"))
   125→            .layer(session_layer)
   126→            .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http())
   127→            .layer(PropagateRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id.clone()))
   128→            .layer(SetRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id, MakeRequestUuid))
   129→            .with_state(pool)
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    pub async fn run(self) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
   133→        let router = Self::router(self.pool);
   134→        axum::serve(self.listener, router).await
   135→    }
   136→}

Now I have a clear picture. Let me plan and implement Step 6. Based on the acceptance criteria:

1. Projects list shows all active projects
2. Each project shows title and count of next actions  
3. User can view a project to see linked next actions and completed actions
4. Projects without a next action are flagged (stalled)
5. User can add a project directly
6. User can add a next action directly to a project
7. User can complete a project
8. User can delete a project
9. Works without JS; HTMX enhances

I'll start with the domain type, then DB migration, repository, service, routes, templates, CSS, and tests. Let me begin with the domain type.

1→use chrono::{DateTime, Utc};
     2→use sqlx::{PgExecutor, PgPool};
     3→use uuid::Uuid;
     4→
     5→use crate::domain::{ContextId, NextAction, NextActionId, TodoTitle, UserId};
     6→
     7→struct NextActionRecord {
     8→    id: Uuid,
     9→    user_id: Uuid,
    10→    context_id: Uuid,
    11→    title: String,
    12→    created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    13→    completed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    14→}
    15→
    16→impl NextActionRecord {
    17→    fn into_domain(self) -> NextAction {
    18→        let id = NextActionId::from_uuid(self.id);
    19→        let user_id = UserId::from_uuid(self.user_id);
    20→        let context_id = ContextId::from_uuid(self.context_id);
    21→        let title = TodoTitle::parse(self.title).expect("stored title should be valid");
    22→
    23→        NextAction::from_parts(
    24→            id,
    25→            user_id,
    26→            context_id,
    27→            title,
    28→            self.created_at,
    29→            self.completed_at,
    30→        )
    31→    }
    32→}
    33→
    34→pub async fn insert_next_action(
    35→    executor: impl PgExecutor<'_>,
    36→    action: &NextAction,
    37→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
    38→    let completed_at: Option<&DateTime<Utc>> = match action {
    39→        NextAction::Completed { completed_at, .. } => Some(completed_at),
    40→        NextAction::Active { .. } => None,
    41→    };
    42→
    43→    sqlx::query!(
    44→        r#"INSERT INTO next_actions (id, user_id, context_id, title, created_at, completed_at)
    45→           VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5 :: timestamptz, $6 :: timestamptz)"#,
    46→        action.id().as_uuid(),
    47→        action.user_id().as_uuid(),
    48→        action.context_id().as_uuid(),
    49→        action.title().as_str(),
    50→        action.created_at() as &DateTime<Utc>,
    51→        completed_at as Option<&DateTime<Utc>>,
    52→    )
    53→    .execute(executor)
    54→    .await?;
    55→    Ok(())
    56→}
    57→
    58→pub async fn find_active_next_actions_by_user(
    59→    pool: &PgPool,
    60→    user_id: &UserId,
    61→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
    62→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    63→        NextActionRecord,
    64→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
    65→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    66→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    67→           FROM next_actions
    68→           WHERE user_id = $1 AND completed_at IS NULL
    69→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    70→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    71→    )
    72→    .fetch_all(pool)
    73→    .await?;
    74→
    75→    Ok(records
    76→        .into_iter()
    77→        .map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain())
    78→        .collect())
    79→}
    80→
    81→pub async fn find_active_next_actions_by_user_and_context(
    82→    pool: &PgPool,
    83→    user_id: &UserId,
    84→    context_id: &ContextId,
    85→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
    86→    let records = sqlx::query_as!(
    87→        NextActionRecord,
    88→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
    89→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
    90→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
    91→           FROM next_actions
    92→           WHERE user_id = $1 AND context_id = $2 AND completed_at IS NULL
    93→           ORDER BY created_at ASC"#,
    94→        user_id.as_uuid(),
    95→        context_id.as_uuid(),
    96→    )
    97→    .fetch_all(pool)
    98→    .await?;
    99→
   100→    Ok(records
   101→        .into_iter()
   102→        .map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain())
   103→        .collect())
   104→}
   105→
   106→pub async fn find_next_action_by_id(
   107→    pool: &PgPool,
   108→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   109→) -> Result<Option<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
   110→    let record: Option<NextActionRecord> = sqlx::query_as!(
   111→        NextActionRecord,
   112→        r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, title,
   113→           created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
   114→           completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
   115→           FROM next_actions WHERE id = $1"#,
   116→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   117→    )
   118→    .fetch_optional(pool)
   119→    .await?;
   120→
   121→    Ok(record.map(|r: NextActionRecord| r.into_domain()))
   122→}
   123→
   124→pub async fn complete_next_action(
   125→    pool: &PgPool,
   126→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   127→    completed_at: &DateTime<Utc>,
   128→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   129→    sqlx::query!(
   130→        r#"UPDATE next_actions SET completed_at = $1 :: timestamptz WHERE id = $2"#,
   131→        completed_at as &DateTime<Utc>,
   132→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   133→    )
   134→    .execute(pool)
   135→    .await?;
   136→    Ok(())
   137→}
   138→
   139→pub async fn delete_next_action(
   140→    pool: &PgPool,
   141→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   142→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   143→    sqlx::query!(
   144→        r#"DELETE FROM next_actions WHERE id = $1"#,
   145→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   146→    )
   147→    .execute(pool)
   148→    .await?;
   149→    Ok(())
   150→}
   151→
   152→pub async fn update_next_action_title(
   153→    pool: &PgPool,
   154→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   155→    title: &TodoTitle,
   156→) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
   157→    sqlx::query!(
   158→        r#"UPDATE next_actions SET title = $1 WHERE id = $2"#,
   159→        title.as_str(),
   160→        action_id.as_uuid(),
   161→    )
   162→    .execute(pool)
   163→    .await?;
   164→    Ok(())
   165→}
   166→
   167→pub async fn count_active_next_actions(
   168→    pool: &PgPool,
   169→    user_id: &UserId,
   170→) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
   171→    let record = sqlx::query!(
   172→        r#"SELECT COUNT(*) as "count!" FROM next_actions WHERE user_id = $1 AND completed_at IS NULL"#,
   173→        user_id.as_uuid(),
   174→    )
   175→    .fetch_one(pool)
   176→    .await?;
   177→
   178→    Ok(record.count)
   179→}
   180→

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Session Start**: This is a continuation session. Luca Palmieri (Driver) was implementing Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash) and had 3 failing tests from template changes. The session summary indicated the tests needed fixing.

2. **Test Fixes (Step 5)**: 
   - Read `tests/api/inbox.rs` to understand the 3 failing tests
   - Fixed `delete_button_has_accessible_label_with_item_title`: Changed assertion from `"Delete: Call the dentist"` to `"Trash: Call the dentist"`
   - Fixed `delete_inbox_item_removes_it_and_redirects`: Changed ID extraction to use `rfind` from the `/delete"` position backwards
   - Fixed `htmx_delete_inbox_item_returns_empty_body_with_announce`: Same ID extraction fix
   - Also fixed formatting issues caught by `cargo fmt`

3. **Pipeline run**: fmt, clippy clean, 154 integration tests pass

4. **Playwright fix**: e2e test also referenced "Delete: Call the dentist" - changed to "Trash: Call the dentist" in `e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` line 276

5. **Glossary update**: Updated `docs/glossary.md` - marked `clarify_as_next_action()` and `Trash` as Implemented, added `ClarifyAsNextActionError` variants

6. **SQLx prepare + commit**: Commit `fa565db` pushed, CI green (run 21966590267)

7. **Multiple stale messages from team-lead**: Received 4+ stale messages asking to update Step 4 docs and start Step 5, even though both were already done. Each time, responded with evidence (git log, commit hashes).

8. **Review broadcast**: Team lead finally broadcast review request. 3/9 approved initially (Marty, Carson, Steve Krug).

9. **Stale Step 4 reviews**: Received reviews from Lea, Carson, Steve Schoger, Scott, Kent, Marty, Heydon - all reviewing Step 4, not Step 5. Redirected each to the correct Step 5 commit.

10. **Two blocking items identified by team-lead**:
    - DB transaction for clarify (Scott + Kent)
    - Focus management after clarify/trash (Heydon)

11. **Blocking fix #1 - DB Transaction**:
    - Updated `src/infrastructure/inbox_repository.rs`: Changed import to include `PgExecutor`, updated `delete_inbox_item` and `find_inbox_item_by_id` to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>`
    - Updated `src/infrastructure/next_action_repository.rs`: Changed import to include `PgExecutor`, updated `insert_next_action` to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>`
    - Updated `src/services/inbox_service.rs`: Wrapped `clarify_as_next_action` in `pool.begin()` / `tx.commit()` transaction

12. **Blocking fix #2 - Focus Management**:
    - Created `static/js/inbox-focus.js` following `next-action-focus.js` pattern
    - Updated `templates/inbox.html`: Always render empty state div (with hidden attr), replaced inline script with external JS file reference
    - Added test `inbox_page_includes_focus_management_script`

13. **Pipeline + Commit**: All 212 tests pass (57 unit + 155 integration) + 11 Playwright e2e. Commit `2ce0d4a` pushed, CI green (run 21966876342).

14. **9/9 consensus on Step 5**: Team lead confirmed.

15. **Mini-retro**: Broadcast to team covering what went well, what could improve, and action items.

16. **Docs update**: Marked Step 5 as [x], moved NEXT to Step 6, updated current step to "6 -- Projects". Commit `410e416` pushed.

17. **Step 6 Implementation Started**:
    - Read US-GTD-4 acceptance criteria
    - Read glossary for Project type (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped)
    - Read existing next_actions migration (has `project_id UUID NULL`)
    - Read existing code structure

18. **Domain type created**: `src/domain/project.rs` with:
    - `ProjectId` newtype
    - `Project` enum (Active, Completed, Dropped)
    - Methods: `new`, `from_parts`, `complete`, `drop_project`, accessors
    - 13 unit tests

19. **Migration created**: `migrations/20240101000007_create_projects_table.sql`
    - Creates `projects` table
    - Adds FK constraint from `next_actions.project_id` to `projects.id`
    - Adds index on `next_actions.project_id`

20. **Project repository created**: `src/infrastructure/project_repository.rs`
    - CRUD operations + count queries + stalled projects count

21. **NextAction domain type updated**: Added `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` to both variants
    - Updated `new`, `from_parts`, `complete` methods
    - Added `new_for_project` constructor
    - Added `project_id()` accessor
    - Updated unit tests for new `from_parts` signature

22. **NextAction repository updated**: Added `project_id` to record struct, all queries, and insert statement. Added `find_next_actions_by_project` query.

23. **Compilation error**: `cargo check` failed because:
    - The `projects` table doesn't exist yet in the running database (SQLx compile-time check)
    - Need to run migrations first before SQLx can verify queries
    - This is where work stopped.

Now let me verify I have all files and changes tracked correctly.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Luca Palmieri (Driver agent) is implementing the GTD todo list application. The session started as a continuation from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The immediate task was to fix 3 failing tests from Step 5 template changes, then complete the Step 5 pipeline (commit, push, CI, review, consensus). After achieving 9/9 consensus on Step 5, the work transitioned to implementing Step 6: Projects (US-GTD-4). The team lead repeatedly (with stale messages) instructed: "Begin Step 6: Projects (US-GTD-4)" and confirmed unanimous driver selection for Luca.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust (nightly) + Axum 0.8 web framework with SQLx 0.8 compile-time checked queries
   - Askama 0.13 templates for server-side rendering
   - HTMX progressive enhancement with focus management protocol
   - Driver-Reviewer mob model with 9/9 consensus requirement
   - Atomic Green Step Pipeline: fmt -> clippy -> test -> playwright -> refactor -> glossary -> commit+push -> CI green -> mini-retro -> consensus
   - `PgExecutor` trait for transaction composability in repository functions
   - Database transactions for multi-entity atomic operations
   - Project domain type as enum state machine: Active -> Completed / Dropped
   - NextAction now has optional `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` linking to projects

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/tests/api/inbox.rs` (MODIFIED)
     - Fixed 3 failing tests caused by Step 5 template changes, added 1 new test for focus management script
     - Changed aria-label assertion from "Delete:" to "Trash:" (line 400)
     - Changed item ID extraction in delete tests to use `rfind` from `/delete"` position backwards instead of forward-scanning `find`
     - Added `inbox_page_includes_focus_management_script` test
     ```rust
     // New ID extraction pattern (used in 2 tests):
     let delete_action_prefix = "action=\"/inbox/";
     let delete_action_pos = body.find("/delete\"").expect("Missing /delete action");
     let action_start = body[..delete_action_pos]
         .rfind(delete_action_prefix)
         .expect("Missing delete form action prefix")
         + delete_action_prefix.len();
     let item_id = &body[action_start..delete_action_pos];
     ```

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/e2e/user-journey.spec.ts` (MODIFIED)
     - Changed line 276 from `"Delete: Call the dentist"` to `"Trash: Call the dentist"` to match new template

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/glossary.md` (MODIFIED)
     - Marked `clarify_as_next_action()` as **Implemented**
     - Marked `Trash` as **Implemented**
     - Added `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` and `ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized` to Domain Errors table

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/services/inbox_service.rs` (MODIFIED)
     - Wrapped `clarify_as_next_action()` in database transaction for atomicity
     ```rust
     pub async fn clarify_as_next_action(
         pool: &PgPool,
         item_id: &InboxItemId,
         user_id: &UserId,
         context_id: ContextId,
     ) -> Result<NextAction, ClarifyAsNextActionError> {
         let mut tx = pool.begin().await.map_err(|e| {
             ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to begin transaction: {e}"))
         })?;
         let item = inbox_repository::find_inbox_item_by_id(&mut *tx, item_id)
             .await
             .map_err(|e| ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
             .ok_or(ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound)?;
         if item.user_id() != user_id {
             return Err(ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized);
         }
         let action = NextAction::new(user_id.clone(), context_id, item.title().clone());
         next_action_repository::insert_next_action(&mut *tx, &action)
             .await
             .map_err(|e| {
                 ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to insert next action: {e}"))
             })?;
         inbox_repository::delete_inbox_item(&mut *tx, item_id)
             .await
             .map_err(|e| {
                 ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to delete inbox item: {e}"))
             })?;
         tx.commit().await.map_err(|e| {
             ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to commit transaction: {e}"))
         })?;
         tracing::info!("Inbox item clarified as next action");
         Ok(action)
     }
     ```

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/infrastructure/inbox_repository.rs` (MODIFIED)
     - Added `PgExecutor` import
     - Changed `delete_inbox_item` and `find_inbox_item_by_id` to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>` instead of `&PgPool`

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/infrastructure/next_action_repository.rs` (MODIFIED)
     - Added `PgExecutor` import, added `ProjectId` import
     - Changed `insert_next_action` to accept `impl PgExecutor<'_>`
     - Added `project_id: Option<Uuid>` to `NextActionRecord`
     - Updated `into_domain()` to map `project_id`
     - Updated `insert_next_action` to include `project_id` in INSERT
     - Updated all SELECT queries to include `project_id`
     - Added `find_next_actions_by_project()` function
     ```rust
     pub async fn find_next_actions_by_project(
         pool: &PgPool,
         project_id: &ProjectId,
     ) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, sqlx::Error> {
         let records = sqlx::query_as!(
             NextActionRecord,
             r#"SELECT id, user_id, context_id, project_id, title,
                created_at as "created_at: DateTime<Utc>",
                completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime<Utc>"
                FROM next_actions
                WHERE project_id = $1
                ORDER BY completed_at NULLS FIRST, created_at ASC"#,
             project_id.as_uuid(),
         )
         .fetch_all(pool)
         .await?;
         Ok(records.into_iter().map(|r| r.into_domain()).collect())
     }
     ```

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/js/inbox-focus.js` (CREATED)
     - Focus management for inbox page following `next-action-focus.js` pattern
     - Handles focus after clarify/trash removes inbox item: next sibling -> previous sibling -> capture input
     - Handles empty state transitions via hidden attribute toggling

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/inbox.html` (MODIFIED)
     - Changed empty state div to always render (hidden when items exist) for JS compatibility
     - Replaced inline script block with `<script src="/static/js/inbox-focus.js"></script>`

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` (MODIFIED)
     - Step 5 marked [x], NEXT marker moved to Step 6, current step updated to "6 -- Projects"

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/project.rs` (CREATED - Step 6)
     - `ProjectId` newtype wrapper with `new()`, `from_uuid()`, `as_uuid()`
     - `Project` enum with Active, Completed, Dropped variants
     - Methods: `new()`, `from_parts()`, `id()`, `user_id()`, `title()`, `created_at()`, `is_active()`, `is_completed()`, `is_dropped()`, `complete()`, `drop_project()`
     - 13 unit tests covering all state transitions and `from_parts` reconstruction

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/mod.rs` (MODIFIED - Step 6)
     - Added `mod project;` and `pub use project::{Project, ProjectId};`

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/next_action.rs` (MODIFIED - Step 6)
     - Added `use super::project::ProjectId;`
     - Added `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` to both Active and Completed variants
     - Added `new_for_project()` constructor
     - Updated `from_parts()` to accept `project_id: Option<ProjectId>` parameter (BREAKING: new parameter between context_id and title)
     - Added `project_id()` accessor returning `Option<&ProjectId>`
     - Updated `complete()` to carry `project_id`
     - Updated unit tests for new `from_parts` signature (added `None` for project_id)

   - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/migrations/20240101000007_create_projects_table.sql` (CREATED - Step 6)
     ```sql
     CREATE TABLE projects (
         id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
         user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
         title TEXT NOT NULL,
         created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
         completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL,
         dropped_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL

517:### US-GTD-4: Manage Projects
518-
519-**As a** GTD practitioner,
520-**I want to** see all my active projects and their next actions,
521-**So that** I can ensure every project is moving forward.
522-
523-#### Acceptance Criteria
524-
525-- [ ] Projects list shows all active projects
526-- [ ] Each project shows its title and the count of active next actions
527-- [ ] Stalled projects (zero next actions) are visually flagged
528-- [ ] User can view a project detail page showing all linked next actions (active and
529-  completed) and waiting-for items
530-- [ ] User can add a project directly (without going through inbox)
531-- [ ] User can add a next action directly to a project (with context selection)
532-- [ ] User can complete a project (marks as finished)
533-- [ ] User can drop a project (abandoned, different from completed)
534-- [ ] User can edit a project's title
535-- [ ] User can delete a project (with confirmation; warns about linked actions)
536-- [ ] Works without JavaScript; HTMX enhances
537-
538-#### Accessibility
539-- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
540-- [ ] Project list uses semantic `<ul>` so screen readers announce item count
541-- [ ] Stalled project flag is communicated via text (not color alone) -- e.g.,
542-  "No next actions" label visible alongside the project title
543-- [ ] Project detail page has clear heading hierarchy (`<h1>` project title,
544-  `<h2>` sections for next actions, completed actions, waiting for)
545-- [ ] Add/complete/delete/edit actions have accessible labels including the project title
546-
547-#### Empty State
548-- No projects: "No projects yet. Projects are outcomes that need more than one step.
549-  You can create one here or during inbox processing." with link to add project form.
550-
551-#### Edge Cases
552-- Project with no next actions (stalled -- flagged during weekly review)
553-- Deleting a project with active next actions (what happens to them?)
554-- Completing a project with remaining active next actions
555-- Very long project title
556-
557-#### Success Metric
558-During weekly review, user can verify all projects have next actions in under 2 minutes.
559-
560----
561-
562-### US-GTD-5: Waiting For List
563-
564-**As a** GTD practitioner,
565-**I want to** track items I'm waiting on from others,
566-**So that** I can follow up at the right time and nothing falls through the cracks.
567-
--
729:6. **Projects** (US-GTD-4) -- Multi-step outcome tracking
730-7. **Clarify as Project** (US-GTD-2d) -- Inbox -> new project + first next action
731-8. **Waiting For** (US-GTD-5) -- Delegation tracking
732-9. **Clarify as Waiting For** (US-GTD-2e) -- Inbox -> waiting for
733-10. **Someday/Maybe** (US-GTD-6) -- Idea parking
734-11. **Clarify as Someday/Maybe** (US-GTD-2c) -- Inbox -> someday/maybe
735-12. **Weekly Review** (US-GTD-7) -- The review process
736-13. **Data Migration + Cleanup** -- Migrate old todos, remove legacy code
737-
738-Each step follows the Atomic Green Step pipeline. Each step delivers a usable increment.
739-The clarify workflow is built incrementally -- each clarify path ships as soon as its
740-destination list is available.
741-
742----
743-
744-## Product Success Criteria (V1 GTD)
745-
746-V1 GTD is successful when:
747-
748-1. A user can capture items to their inbox and process them to zero
749-2. Clarified items land in the correct list (Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For,
750-   Someday/Maybe, or trash)
751-3. Next Actions are filterable by context
752-4. Every active project has at least one next action (stalled projects are flagged)
753-5. The Weekly Review guided flow keeps the system current
754-6. The entire system is accessible (WCAG 2.2 AA)
755-7. The system works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)
756-8. A first-time GTD user can understand the system without external instructions
757-9. All existing auth functionality continues to work
758-10. All tests pass (Rust integration + Playwright e2e + axe-core a11y)
759-
760-We will know this is working when a user can complete the full GTD loop (capture ->
761-clarify -> organize -> review -> engage) without hesitation.

1→use sqlx::PgPool;
     2→
     3→use crate::domain::{ContextId, NextAction, NextActionId, TodoTitle, TodoTitleError, UserId};
     4→use crate::infrastructure::next_action_repository;
     5→
     6→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
     7→pub enum AddNextActionError {
     8→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
     9→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
    10→    #[error(transparent)]
    11→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    12→}
    13→
    14→#[tracing::instrument(
    15→    name = "add_next_action",
    16→    skip(pool, title),
    17→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid(), context_id = %context_id.as_uuid())
    18→)]
    19→pub async fn add_next_action(
    20→    pool: &PgPool,
    21→    user_id: UserId,
    22→    context_id: ContextId,
    23→    title: String,
    24→) -> Result<NextAction, AddNextActionError> {
    25→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(title)?;
    26→    let action = NextAction::new(user_id, context_id, title);
    27→
    28→    next_action_repository::insert_next_action(pool, &action)
    29→        .await
    30→        .map_err(|e| {
    31→            AddNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to insert next action: {e}"))
    32→        })?;
    33→
    34→    tracing::info!("Next action added");
    35→    Ok(action)
    36→}
    37→
    38→#[tracing::instrument(
    39→    name = "list_active_next_actions",
    40→    skip(pool),
    41→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    42→)]
    43→pub async fn list_active_next_actions(
    44→    pool: &PgPool,
    45→    user_id: &UserId,
    46→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, anyhow::Error> {
    47→    let actions = next_action_repository::find_active_next_actions_by_user(pool, user_id)
    48→        .await
    49→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch next actions: {e}"))?;
    50→
    51→    Ok(actions)
    52→}
    53→
    54→#[tracing::instrument(
    55→    name = "list_active_next_actions_by_context",
    56→    skip(pool),
    57→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid(), context_id = %context_id.as_uuid())
    58→)]
    59→pub async fn list_active_next_actions_by_context(
    60→    pool: &PgPool,
    61→    user_id: &UserId,
    62→    context_id: &ContextId,
    63→) -> Result<Vec<NextAction>, anyhow::Error> {
    64→    let actions = next_action_repository::find_active_next_actions_by_user_and_context(
    65→        pool, user_id, context_id,
    66→    )
    67→    .await
    68→    .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to fetch next actions by context: {e}"))?;
    69→
    70→    Ok(actions)
    71→}
    72→
    73→#[tracing::instrument(
    74→    name = "count_active_next_actions",
    75→    skip(pool),
    76→    fields(user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
    77→)]
    78→pub async fn count_active_next_actions(
    79→    pool: &PgPool,
    80→    user_id: &UserId,
    81→) -> Result<i64, anyhow::Error> {
    82→    let count = next_action_repository::count_active_next_actions(pool, user_id)
    83→        .await
    84→        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to count next actions: {e}"))?;
    85→
    86→    Ok(count)
    87→}
    88→
    89→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
    90→pub enum CompleteNextActionError {
    91→    #[error("Next action not found")]
    92→    NotFound,
    93→    #[error("Not authorized to complete this next action")]
    94→    Unauthorized,
    95→    #[error(transparent)]
    96→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
    97→}
    98→
    99→#[tracing::instrument(
   100→    name = "complete_next_action",
   101→    skip(pool),
   102→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   103→)]
   104→pub async fn complete_next_action(
   105→    pool: &PgPool,
   106→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   107→    user_id: &UserId,
   108→) -> Result<(), CompleteNextActionError> {
   109→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   110→        .await
   111→        .map_err(|e| CompleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   112→        .ok_or(CompleteNextActionError::NotFound)?;
   113→
   114→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   115→        return Err(CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized);
   116→    }
   117→
   118→    let completed = action.complete();
   119→    if let NextAction::Completed { completed_at, .. } = &completed {
   120→        next_action_repository::complete_next_action(pool, action_id, completed_at)
   121→            .await
   122→            .map_err(|e| {
   123→                CompleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   124→                    "Failed to complete next action: {e}"
   125→                ))
   126→            })?;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    tracing::info!("Next action completed");
   130→    Ok(())
   131→}
   132→
   133→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   134→pub enum DeleteNextActionError {
   135→    #[error("Next action not found")]
   136→    NotFound,
   137→    #[error("Not authorized to delete this next action")]
   138→    Unauthorized,
   139→    #[error(transparent)]
   140→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   141→}
   142→
   143→#[tracing::instrument(
   144→    name = "delete_next_action",
   145→    skip(pool),
   146→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   147→)]
   148→pub async fn delete_next_action(
   149→    pool: &PgPool,
   150→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   151→    user_id: &UserId,
   152→) -> Result<(), DeleteNextActionError> {
   153→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   154→        .await
   155→        .map_err(|e| DeleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?
   156→        .ok_or(DeleteNextActionError::NotFound)?;
   157→
   158→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   159→        return Err(DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized);
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    next_action_repository::delete_next_action(pool, action_id)
   163→        .await
   164→        .map_err(|e| {
   165→            DeleteNextActionError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to delete next action: {e}"))
   166→        })?;
   167→
   168→    tracing::info!("Next action deleted");
   169→    Ok(())
   170→}
   171→
   172→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
   173→pub enum UpdateNextActionTitleError {
   174→    #[error("Next action not found")]
   175→    NotFound,
   176→    #[error("Not authorized to edit this next action")]
   177→    Unauthorized,
   178→    #[error("Invalid title: {0}")]
   179→    InvalidTitle(#[from] TodoTitleError),
   180→    #[error(transparent)]
   181→    Unexpected(#[from] anyhow::Error),
   182→}
   183→
   184→#[tracing::instrument(
   185→    name = "update_next_action_title",
   186→    skip(pool, new_title),
   187→    fields(action_id = %action_id.as_uuid(), user_id = %user_id.as_uuid())
   188→)]
   189→pub async fn update_next_action_title(
   190→    pool: &PgPool,
   191→    action_id: &NextActionId,
   192→    user_id: &UserId,
   193→    new_title: String,
   194→) -> Result<(), UpdateNextActionTitleError> {
   195→    let action = next_action_repository::find_next_action_by_id(pool, action_id)
   196→        .await
   197→        .map_err(|e| {
   198→            UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}"))
   199→        })?
   200→        .ok_or(UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound)?;
   201→
   202→    if action.user_id() != user_id {
   203→        return Err(UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized);
   204→    }
   205→
   206→    let title = TodoTitle::parse(new_title)?;
   207→
   208→    next_action_repository::update_next_action_title(pool, action_id, &title)
   209→        .await
   210→        .map_err(|e| {
   211→            UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!(
   212→                "Failed to update next action title: {e}"
   213→            ))
   214→        })?;
   215→
   216→    tracing::info!("Next action title updated");
   217→    Ok(())
   218→}

    10→
    11→use crate::configuration::Settings;
    12→use crate::routes::{
    13→    get_contexts, get_dashboard, get_edit_context, get_edit_next_action, get_edit_todo,
    14→    get_forgot_password, get_inbox, get_login, get_next_action_item, get_next_actions,

    22→pub struct Application {
    23→    listener: TcpListener,
    24→    pool: PgPool,
    25→}
    26→
    27→impl Application {
    28→    pub async fn build(settings: &Settings) -> Result<Self, anyhow::Error> {
    29→        let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
    30→            .max_connections(5)
    31→            .connect_with(settings.database.connect_options())
    32→            .await?;
    33→
    34→        sqlx::migrate!("./migrations").run(&pool).await?;
    35→
    36→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    37→        session_store.migrate().await?;
    38→
    39→        let address = format!(
    40→            "{}:{}",
    41→            settings.application.host, settings.application.port
    42→        );
    43→        let listener = TcpListener::bind(&address).await?;
    44→
    45→        tracing::info!("Listening on {}", listener.local_addr()?);
    46→
    47→        Ok(Self { listener, pool })
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    pub fn port(&self) -> u16 {
    51→        self.listener.local_addr().unwrap().port()
    52→    }
    53→
    54→    pub fn router(pool: PgPool) -> Router {
    55→        let x_request_id = axum::http::HeaderName::from_static("x-request-id");
    56→
    57→        let session_store = PostgresStore::new(pool.clone());
    58→        let session_layer = SessionManagerLayer::new(session_store);
    59→
    60→        Router::new()
    61→            .route("/", axum::routing::get(index))
    62→            .route("/dashboard", axum::routing::get(get_dashboard))
    63→            .route("/inbox", axum::routing::get(get_inbox).post(post_inbox))
    64→            .route(
    65→                "/inbox/{id}/delete",
    66→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_inbox_item),
    67→            )
    68→            .route(
    69→                "/inbox/{id}/clarify",
    70→                axum::routing::post(post_clarify_inbox_item),
    71→            )
    72→            .route(
    73→                "/contexts",
    74→                axum::routing::get(get_contexts).post(post_context),
    75→            )
    76→            .route(
    77→                "/contexts/{id}/edit",
    78→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_context).post(post_edit_context),
    79→            )
    80→            .route(
    81→                "/contexts/{id}/delete",
    82→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_context),
    83→            )
    84→            .route(
    85→                "/next-actions",
    86→                axum::routing::get(get_next_actions).post(post_next_action),
    87→            )
    88→            .route(
    89→                "/next-actions/{id}",
    90→                axum::routing::get(get_next_action_item),
    91→            )
    92→            .route(
    93→                "/next-actions/{id}/complete",
    94→                axum::routing::post(post_complete_next_action),
    95→            )
    96→            .route(
    97→                "/next-actions/{id}/delete",
    98→                axum::routing::post(post_delete_next_action),
    99→            )
   100→            .route(
   101→                "/next-actions/{id}/edit",
   102→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_next_action).post(post_edit_next_action),
   103→            )
   104→            .route("/projects", axum::routing::get(get_projects))
   105→            .route("/waiting-for", axum::routing::get(get_waiting_for))
   106→            .route("/someday-maybe", axum::routing::get(get_someday_maybe))
   107→            .route("/review", axum::routing::get(get_review))
   108→            .route(
   109→                "/register",
   110→                axum::routing::get(get_register).post(post_register),
   111→            )
   112→            .route("/login", axum::routing::get(get_login).post(post_login))
   113→            .route("/forgot-password", axum::routing::get(get_forgot_password))
   114→            .route("/logout", axum::routing::post(post_logout))
   115→            .route("/todos", axum::routing::get(get_todos_page).post(post_todo))
   116→            .route("/todos/{id}", axum::routing::get(get_todo_item))
   117→            .route("/todos/{id}/toggle", axum::routing::post(post_toggle_todo))
   118→            .route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo))
   119→            .route(
   120→                "/todos/{id}/edit",
   121→                axum::routing::get(get_edit_todo).post(post_edit_todo),
   122→            )
   123→            .route("/health_check", axum::routing::get(health_check))
   124→            .nest_service("/static", ServeDir::new("static"))
   125→            .layer(session_layer)
   126→            .layer(TraceLayer::new_for_http())
   127→            .layer(PropagateRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id.clone()))
   128→            .layer(SetRequestIdLayer::new(x_request_id, MakeRequestUuid))
   129→            .with_state(pool)
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    pub async fn run(self) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
   133→        let router = Self::router(self.pool);
   134→        axum::serve(self.listener, router).await
   135→    }
   136→}

content.content:
1→# Domain Glossary
     2→
     3→Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our GTD (Getting
     4→Things Done) application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business
     5→says "capture to inbox," the code says `capture_to_inbox()`.
     6→
     7→Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Core Domain Types
    12→
    13→| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
    14→|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
    15→| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
    16→| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
    17→| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
    18→| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
    19→| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
    20→| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
    21→| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
    22→| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
    23→| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |
    24→
    25→## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
    26→
    27→| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
    28→|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
    29→| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
    30→| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
    31→| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
    32→| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
    33→| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
    34→| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
    35→| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
    36→| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
    37→| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
    38→| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |
    39→
    40→## Domain Errors
    41→
    42→| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
    43→|-----------------|-----------|-------|
    44→| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
    45→| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
    46→| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    47→| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    48→| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    49→| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    50→| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
    51→| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
    52→| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
    53→| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
    54→| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
    55→| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
    56→| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |
    57→| Context name empty | `ContextNameError::Empty` | Context name cannot be blank |
    58→| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
    59→| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
    60→| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    61→| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    62→| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    63→| Not authorized (delete next action) | `DeleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    64→| Next action not found (edit) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
    65→| Not authorized (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
    66→| Invalid title (edit next action) | `UpdateNextActionTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_next_action_title service |
    67→| Inbox item not found (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |
    68→| Not authorized (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the inbox item |
    69→
    70→## Error Copy Convention
    71→
    72→Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
    73→are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
    74→tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.
    75→
    76→The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
    77→call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.
    78→
    79→| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
    80→|-------------|-----------------|
    81→| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
    82→| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
    83→| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
    84→| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
    85→| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
    86→| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
    87→| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
    88→| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
    89→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add) | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
    90→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit) | "Title cannot be empty" |
    91→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
    92→
    93→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (add next action) | (silently ignored -- empty submissions are not errors) |
    94→| `TodoTitleError::Empty` (edit next action) | "Title cannot be empty" |
    95→| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` (next action) | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |
    96→
    97→*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## Type Design Principles
   102→
   103→These principles govern how we model the domain:
   104→
   105→1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   106→   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   107→   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.
   108→
   109→2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   110→   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   111→   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   112→   inconsistent.
   113→
   114→3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   115→   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   116→   abstractions in Rust.
   117→
   118→4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   119→   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   120→   I/O has leaked into the domain.
   121→
   122→5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   123→   ```
   124→   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   125→   ```
   126→   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.
   127→
   128→## State Machine: TodoItem
   129→
   130→```
   131→                 add_todo()
   132→  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
   133→                                 │  ^
   134→               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
   135→                                 │  │
   136→                                 v  │
   137→                             Completed
   138→```
   139→
   140→Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
   141→The `update_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
   142→Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).
   143→
   144→---
   145→
   146→## GTD Domain Types (V1 -- Approved)
   147→
   148→*These types will replace the simple TodoItem model as we evolve to GTD. Approved by
   149→team consensus. Final type signatures subject to domain architect guidance during
   150→implementation.*
   151→
   152→### Core GTD Concepts
   153→
   154→| Domain Term | Proposed Rust Type | Notes |
   155→|-------------|-------------------|-------|
   156→| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
   157→| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
   158→| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
   159→| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
   160→| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
   161→| Context ID | `ContextId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a context. **Implemented.** |
   162→| Waiting For item | `WaitingForItem` | Delegated/blocked item. Has title, waiting_on (text), and date added. |
   163→| Someday/Maybe item | `SomedayMaybeItem` | Parked idea. Has title and created_at. Not committed to. |
   164→| Item title | `ItemTitle` | Replaces `TodoTitle`. Non-empty, max 300 chars, trimmed. Same validation. |
   165→| Context name | `ContextName` | Non-empty, max 50 chars, trimmed. Validated at construction via `ContextName::parse()`. **Implemented.** |
   166→
   167→### GTD Actions (Ubiquitous Language)
   168→
   169→| Business Action | Proposed Rust Function / Method | Notes |
   170→|-----------------|-------------------------------|-------|
   171→| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
   172→| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
   173→| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
   174→| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
   175→| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
   176→| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
   177→| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
   178→| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
   179→| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
   180→| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
   181→| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
   182→| Drop project | `Project::drop()` | Active -> Dropped; project abandoned |
   183→| Activate someday/maybe | `activate()` | SomedayMaybeItem -> InboxItem (for re-clarification) |
   184→| Resolve waiting for | `resolve()` | WaitingForItem -> completed or moved to inbox |
   185→| Add context | `add_context()` | Creates a new user-defined context |
   186→| Start weekly review | `start_review()` | Initiates the three-phase review flow |
   187→
   188→### GTD State Machines
   189→
   190→**InboxItem Lifecycle:**
   191→```
   192→                capture()
   193→  [nothing] ──────────────> InboxItem
   194→                                │
   195→                      clarify() │
   196→              ┌────────┬────────┼────────┬────────┐
   197→              v        v        v        v        v
   198→         NextAction  Project  WaitingFor  Someday  [trash]
   199→```
   200→
   201→**NextAction Lifecycle:**
   202→```
   203→                  clarify_as_next_action()
   204→  InboxItem ─────────────────────────────> NextAction (Active)
   205→                                               │
   206→                                   complete()  │
   207→                                               v
   208→                                         NextAction (Completed)
   209→```
   210→
   211→**Project Lifecycle:**
   212→```
   213→                    clarify_as_project()
   214→  InboxItem ──────────────────────────> Project (Active)
   215→                                           │  │
   216→                              complete()   │  │  drop()
   217→                                           v  v
   218→                                    Completed / Dropped
   219→```
   220→
